summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates host information to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint at install time
details: The package's package.json declares a lifecycle script that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers host identifiers (hostname, cwd, environment variables) and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A second module, scanner.js, expands on this: it imports fs, os, https, and child_process; walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync against multiple paths; runs execSync (including `whoami`) to gather host/user identity (os.hostname(), os.platform()); reads process.env; and POSTs the collected data via https.request to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The destination is a GitHub Pages subdomain unrelated to any documented publisher of a 'chain key validator' utility, and the package's stated purpose has no need to enumerate the installer's filesystem, run shell commands, or transmit host identity off-machine. This combination — install/lifecycle-time hardcoded C2 + system-information harvesting + filesystem enumeration + shell execution for identity gathering — is a credential/host-intelligence exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
